title: Intricately Roasted Ethiopian Cornmeal Dabo
description: A traditional Ethiopian dish made with cornmeal and roasted to perfection in a wood-fired oven, this dabo is a fluffy and festive treat, friendly to gluten-free diets.
instructions: Preheat the wood-fired oven to 425 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together the cornmeal, salt, and baking powder.
Gradually add in the water, stirring with a wooden spoon until the mixture forms a smooth batter.
Heat the vegetable oil in a skillet over medium heat and saute the chopped onion until translucent.
Add the minced garlic and berbere spice to the skillet and cook for an additional minute.
Stir the skillet contents into the cornmeal batter until well combined.
Pour the batter into a greased 9-inch round cake pan or a traditional Ethiopian clay mitad.
Place the pan in the preheated wood-fired oven and roast for 30-40 minutes, or until the dabo is golden brown and firm to the touch.
Remove the dabo from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before slicing and serving.
